destinations.evidence: Canonical commitments store explicit commit block and contract expiry metadata.
destinations.snippet: commit_block = new_tip.number(); contract_expiry_block = commit_block.checked_add(async_commitment.ttl)...; add_committed_async_tx(..., commit_block, contract_expiry_block)

destinations.evidence: Async pool expiry is metadata-driven, inclusive through contract_expiry_block, and two-phase deadlines use checked addition.
destinations.snippet: AsyncTxMetadata { commit_block, max_poll_block, contract_expiry_block }; current_block <= contract_expiry_block; settlement_block.checked_add(phase2_offset)

destinations.evidence: Verifier simulation metadata is not the authoritative expiry source.
destinations.snippet: Simulation validates call count/precompile/input, not expiry authority.

destinations.evidence: Dependency execution no longer treats commit_block as an authoritative transaction field.
destinations.snippet: commit_block was removed - the contract now uses block.number for expiry calculation.

equivalence_argument: The old detector behavior is split across payload/verification and transaction-pool metadata; expiry authority lives in AsyncTxMetadata, not simulated call metadata or transaction execution fields.

related_but_insufficient_code: revm AsyncContext carries block_number for SPC creation, but not pool expiry; Alloy TxAsyncCommitment has ttl/precompile data but no commit_block expiry field.

notes: Mapped in port async pool and canonical processing.
rationale: The async expiry candidates evolve from a direct commit_block + ttl formula to stored contract_expiry_block metadata and Phase 2 offset-based pool deadlines, while preserving that verifier re-simulation commit_block is the simulation target block, not the expiry source.
statement: Async pool entries must carry contract-sourced expiry metadata when added; Phase 1 finalized commitments set contract_expiry_block to finalized block plus ttl, Phase 2 pool deadlines are conservatively approximated as commit_block plus the extracted offset with checked addition, and detector commit_block remains the simulation block used for verifier re-simulation rather than the authoritative expiry source.
suggested_falsification_tests: Process a finalized async commitment at block N with ttl T and assert pool metadata stores contract_expiry_block N+T and expires only after that inclusive boundary; force missing expiry metadata and assert the entry is treated as expired/invariant-violating.
Insert two-phase commitments with known commit blocks and offsets and assert max_poll_block equals commit_block + offset, with overflow producing no derived deadline rather than wrapping.

Create an async commitment simulated at target block T but included at block I and assert verifier re-simulation uses T while expiry/deadline checks use contract or pool deadline metadata rather than treating T as the inclusion block.
summary: Async pool expiry is metadata-driven in the port.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Canonicalize commitment at block N/ttl T, assert expiry N+T and replay eligibility through expiry; settle two-phase at S with offset O and assert checked S+O extension.
title: Async pool expiry is metadata-driven
